title: Add TC-FC-020 fixture: missing utterance_fragment defaults to original utterance

Acceptance criteria: fixture added and importable; covers the missing-field case for utterance_fragment default.

title: Write P0/P1 unit tests for confidence threshold routing
description:
Implement unit tests for the core confidence tier dispatch logic (TC-FC-001 through TC-FC-010 from notes/adc-1dj-flag-check-test-cases.md).

Acceptance criteria:
- test/test_flag_check.py covers:
  - TC-FC-001 to TC-FC-003: High confidence (>=0.9) dispatches immediately without clarification flag
  - TC-FC-004 to TC-FC-006: Medium confidence (0.7–0.9) dispatches but is flagged for clarification
  - TC-FC-007 to TC-FC-009: Low confidence (<0.7) returns intent_type=CLARIFICATION
  - TC-FC-010: Missing confidence field defaults to 0.8
- All tests use fixtures from test/fixtures/flag_check_fixtures.py (child adc-tg9)
- Tests mock the LLM client so no real API calls are made
- All tests pass with pytest

References: src/intent/router.py:164-289 (classify_utterance, route_utterance)

title: Locate the project root directory
description: Identify and record the absolute path of the project root directory (the directory containing the src/ folder). Acceptance criteria: running pwd or stat on the identified path succeeds and the path ends with the expected project directory name.

title: Add edge case fixtures for confidence validation and LLM parsing tests
description:
Add fixtures to test/fixtures/flag_check_fixtures.py for TC-FC-011 through TC-FC-018 edge cases.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add INVALID_CONFIDENCE_STRING fixture: LLMResponseMock with confidence='high' (string instead of float)
- Add NEGATIVE_CONFIDENCE fixture: LLMResponseMock with confidence=-0.1
- Add OVER_UNITY_CONFIDENCE fixture: LLMResponseMock with confidence=1.5
- Add UNKNOWN_INTENT_TYPE fixture: LLMResponseMock with intent_type='unknown_type'
- Add EMPTY_JSON_ARRAY fixture: LLMResponseMock with json_data=[]
- Add MIXED_CONFIDENCE_MULTI fixture: LLMResponseMock with 3-intent list (high status, low action, medium lookup)
- Add NON_JSON_RESPONSE fixture: LLMResponseMock with raw_response='I cannot understand that request'
- Add INVALID_JSON_STRUCTURE fixture: LLMResponseMock wrapping a dict (not list) as json_data
- All fixtures importable from test/fixtures/flag_check_fixtures.py
References: test/fixtures/flag_check_fixtures.py, notes/adc-1dj-flag-check-test-cases.md

title: Verify TC-FC-020 code path: missing utterance_fragment falls back to original utterance
description: Identify TC-FC-020 (missing utterance_fragment in LLM response). Confirm that when the LLM omits utterance_fragment from the JSON dict, router.py:230's dict.get fallback supplies the original utterance argument. Acceptance criteria: TC-FC-020 scenario described, fallback confirmed via code trace or test execution, no other path overrides the default.

title: Write tests for urgency field integration (TC-FC-025)
description: Implement unit tests for urgency field behavior on low-confidence intents. Acceptance criteria: TC-FC-025 (low-confidence clarification intent with urgency=critical preserves the urgency field: classification.urgency == 'critical' even when confidence < 0.7). Uses fixtures from test/fixtures/flag_check_fixtures.py. Tests call classify_utterance() with mocked LLM returning clarification + critical urgency. All tests pass with pytest. References: src/intent/router.py:164-252 (classify_utterance)

title: Write P3 unit tests for task-profile routing, urgency, and boundary values
description:
Implement unit tests for task-profile routing, session context, urgency integration, and float boundary values (TC-FC-019 through TC-FC-027 from notes/adc-1dj-flag-check-test-cases.md).

Acceptance criteria:
- test/test_flag_check.py (or separate test/test_flag_check_advanced.py) covers:
  - TC-FC-020: Missing utterance_fragment defaults to original utterance
  - TC-FC-021: task-profile intent with high confidence escalates to bead
  - TC-FC-022: task-profile intent with low confidence still escalates (always escalates)
  - TC-FC-025: Low-confidence critical-urgency intent preserves urgency field
  - TC-FC-026: Missing urgency defaults to 'normal'
  - TC-FC-027: Float precision at boundaries (0.899999 flags, 0.900001 dispatches, 0.699999 clarifies, 0.700001 flags)
- Uses fixtures from test/fixtures/flag_check_fixtures.py (child adc-tg9)
- All tests pass with pytest

Note: TC-FC-019 (LLM timeout), TC-FC-023/024 (session context) are P3 stretch goals — include if implementation is straightforward, skip if they require significant new infrastructure.

References: src/intent/router.py:254-311 (route_utterance, process_intent)

title: Write tests for task-profile always-escalates routing (TC-FC-021, TC-FC-022)
description: Implement unit tests for task-profile intent routing behavior. Acceptance criteria: TC-FC-021 (task-profile intent with high confidence 0.95 escalates to NEEDLE bead, escalate handler called) and TC-FC-022 (task-profile intent with low confidence 0.5 still escalates, confidence threshold is bypassed for task-profile type). Uses fixtures from test/fixtures/flag_check_fixtures.py. Tests call process_intent() with mocked escalate handler. All tests pass with pytest. References: src/intent/router.py:291-311 (process_intent)

title: Verify src/intent/ subdirectory exists and is accessible
description: Confirm that the src/intent/ subdirectory is present within src/ and is readable. Acceptance criteria: running stat or ls on <project-root>/src/intent/ returns successfully without error and shows it is a directory with at least one file.

title: Document all keyword arguments of IntentClassification at router.py:226

The 6 fields are: intent_type, project_slug, confidence, utterance_fragment, reasoning, urgency. Acceptance criteria: all 6 fields documented with value expressions and defaults.

title: Write tests for default field behavior (TC-FC-020, TC-FC-026)
description: Implement unit tests for missing/default field handling in IntentClassification. Acceptance criteria: TC-FC-020 (missing utterance_fragment defaults to original utterance) and TC-FC-026 (missing urgency defaults to normal). Uses fixtures from test/fixtures/flag_check_fixtures.py. All tests pass with pytest. References: src/intent/router.py:164-252

title: Write tests for unknown intent type and empty/multi-intent responses (TC-FC-014, TC-FC-015, TC-FC-016)
description:
Implement unit tests for unknown intent handling and response cardinality edge cases in test/test_flag_check_edge.py.

Acceptance criteria:
- TC-FC-014: Unknown intent_type ('unknown_type') — assert result defaults to STATUS and a warning is logged
- TC-FC-015: Empty JSON array from LLM — assert classify_utterance returns an empty list (no error)
- TC-FC-016: Multiple intents with mixed confidence levels — assert all three intents are returned and each is routed to the correct bucket (high/medium/low) per confidence value
- Uses fixtures UNKNOWN_INTENT_TYPE, EMPTY_JSON_ARRAY, MIXED_CONFIDENCE_MULTI from test/fixtures/flag_check_fixtures.py (child adc-1qw)
- All tests pass with pytest
References: src/intent/router.py:164-252, test/fixtures/flag_check_fixtures.py

title: Write P2 unit tests for edge cases and error conditions
description:
Implement unit tests for edge cases and error handling (TC-FC-011 through TC-FC-018 from notes/adc-1dj-flag-check-test-cases.md).

Acceptance criteria:
- test/test_flag_check.py (or test/test_flag_check_edge.py) covers:
  - TC-FC-011: Invalid confidence string ('high') — ValueError or conversion
  - TC-FC-012: Negative confidence — error handling or clamping
  - TC-FC-013: Over-unity confidence (>1.0) — error handling or clamping
  - TC-FC-014: Unknown intent_type defaults to STATUS with warning logged
  - TC-FC-015: Empty JSON array from LLM returns empty classification list
  - TC-FC-016: Multiple intents with mixed confidence levels — all routed correctly
  - TC-FC-017: Non-JSON LLM response triggers fallback (intent_type=STATUS, confidence=0.5)
  - TC-FC-018: Invalid JSON structure triggers fallback (intent_type=STATUS, confidence=0.5)
- Uses fixtures from test/fixtures/flag_check_fixtures.py (child adc-tg9)
- All tests pass with pytest

References: src/intent/router.py:164-252 (classify_utterance error paths)

title: Write tests for confidence value edge cases (TC-FC-011, TC-FC-012, TC-FC-013)
description:
Implement unit tests for invalid/out-of-range confidence values in test/test_flag_check_edge.py.

Acceptance criteria:
- TC-FC-011: Invalid confidence string ('high') — assert ValueError is raised or value is converted/coerced without crash
- TC-FC-012: Negative confidence (-0.1) — assert error is raised or value is clamped to 0.0
- TC-FC-013: Over-unity confidence (1.5) — assert error is raised or value is clamped to 1.0
- Uses fixtures INVALID_CONFIDENCE_STRING, NEGATIVE_CONFIDENCE, OVER_UNITY_CONFIDENCE from test/fixtures/flag_check_fixtures.py (child adc-1qw)
- Mocks classify_utterance via AsyncMock returning fixture data
- All tests pass with pytest
References: src/intent/router.py:164-252 (classify_utterance error paths), test/fixtures/flag_check_fixtures.py

title: Write tests for LLM parsing failure fallback (TC-FC-017, TC-FC-018)
description:
Implement unit tests for non-JSON and invalid JSON structure LLM responses in test/test_flag_check_edge.py.

Acceptance criteria:
- TC-FC-017: Non-JSON LLM response (plain text) — assert fallback is returned: intent_type=STATUS, confidence=0.5
- TC-FC-018: Invalid JSON structure (dict instead of list) — assert fallback is returned: intent_type=STATUS, confidence=0.5
- Uses fixtures NON_JSON_RESPONSE, INVALID_JSON_STRUCTURE from test/fixtures/flag_check_fixtures.py (child adc-1qw)
- All tests in test/test_flag_check_edge.py pass with pytest (covering TC-FC-011 through TC-FC-018)
- No regressions in test/test_flag_check.py (TC-FC-001 through TC-FC-010)
References: src/intent/router.py:164-252, test/fixtures/flag_check_fixtures.py

Acceptance criteria: TC-FC-027 tests four boundary inputs: confidence=0.899999 (flags, not dispatches), confidence=0.900001 (dispatches immediately), confidence=0.699999 (returns clarification), confidence=0.700001 (flags, not clarifies). Each assertion verifies the correct routing branch is taken. Uses fixtures from test/fixtures/flag_check_fixtures.py. All tests pass with pytest.
